title: Sign-Changing Dirac Mass Supports Chiral Edge Mode
summary: A 2+1-dimensional Dirac system whose mass changes sign across a boundary supports a boundary-localized chiral mode; source-grade Jackiw-Rebbi zero-mode and anomaly-inflow results now make the continuum edge-mode consistency story explicit locally.
assumptions: The bulk effective action is the integer-level Chern-Simons term after doubling.
The folded boundary problem is equivalent to a sign-changing-mass Dirac equation.
regime: Continuum edge-mode realization of the integer quantum Hall boundary theory.
scope: Flagship Lecture Three theorem joining the parity anomaly, Jackiw-Rebbi wall mode, and anomaly-inflow consistency.

source_anchors.notes: Witten's continuum proof of edge-mode consistency from the parity anomaly and a sign-changing mass.
Original zero-mode ingredient, now represented by a dedicated source-grade theorem in the KB.
Original anomaly-inflow ingredient, now represented by a dedicated source-grade theorem in the KB.
Lattice realization of the same continuum consistency story.

representation: Canonical theorem family for the continuum edge-mode realization of the integer Hall boundary.
motivation: This note exists because Lecture Three has its own flagship theorem: the bulk Hall response together with a sign-changing Dirac mass produces a boundary-localized chiral mode. The proof is only clear when the parity-anomaly, Jackiw-Rebbi, and anomaly-inflow pieces are kept explicit.
prerequisite_story: Know the half-level response of one massive Dirac fermion, the folded sign-changing-mass wall equation, the Jackiw-Rebbi zero-mode theorem, and the Callan-Harvey anomaly-inflow identity.
local_context_story: In Witten Lecture Three, this is the culmination of the boundary discussion. Doubling cancels the bulk half anomaly, folding turns the boundary into a sign-changing-mass problem, and the resulting wall mode restores consistency with the integer bulk response.
forward_links_story: This theorem is the boundary companion of the Lecture Two Hall-response theorem: the same integer k that labels the bulk Hall term also fixes the chirality that the boundary mode must carry.
canonical_questions: Which steps connect the parity anomaly to the final chiral boundary mode?
Why does the sign-changing mass matter rather than only the existence of a boundary?
How does the boundary mode restore consistency with the integer bulk Hall response?
scope_boundaries: This theorem is a continuum bulk-boundary consistency theorem and should not be conflated with the distinct lattice no-go branch.
The theorem assumes the integer bulk Hall response has already been identified; it does not rederive the Hall integer from scratch.

math_expressions.notes: One massive Dirac fermion carries a half-level parity anomaly.
Boundary problem rewritten as a sign-changing-mass Dirac equation.
Chiral boundary mode localized at the wall.

faithfulness_strategy: Keep the trusted theorem limited to the continuum bulk-boundary consistency statement proved from sign-changing mass, Jackiw-Rebbi localization, and anomaly inflow, while leaving the lattice no-go branch outside the theorem.
faithfulness_notes: The KB expands the argument into source-grade subclaims but keeps the trusted target theorem at the same scope as the source.
comparator_risks: Do not let the continuum theorem drift into a claim about lattice charge cancellation or species doubling.
attribution_requirements: Retain attribution to Witten for the theorem statement and to Jackiw-Rebbi / Callan-Harvey for the explicit neighboring source-grade ingredients.
admissible_assumptions: Work in the continuum doubled 2+1-dimensional Dirac model with a folded boundary producing a sign-changing mass profile.
Treat the parity-anomaly half level, Jackiw-Rebbi zero mode, and anomaly-inflow cancellation as explicit local sublemmas.
formalization_blockers: A reusable Lean-side analytic interface for domain-wall Dirac operators and localized zero modes is still missing.
The continuum edge-mode theorem is still intentionally separated from the distinct lattice regularization story and needs a formal bridge layer if those branches are to be combined.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks for the flagship Lecture Three boundary-mode theorem, including how the bulk topological term and edge mode fit together.
